# Errors index

Every custom error the Catalog contracts can revert with, its contract, and the condition that raises it.

**`AddressEmptyCode`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
Inherited from OpenZeppelin `Address`.

**`ContractAlreadyRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`addContract`/`addContractFor` targeted a contract pointer already in the artist's catalog.

**`ContractNotRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`removeContract`/`removeContractFor` targeted a contract pointer not in the artist's catalog.

**`FailedCall`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
Inherited from OpenZeppelin `Multicall`/`Address`.

**`InvalidArtist`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
A `*For` function was given the zero address as `artist`.

**`InvalidContractAddress`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
A pointer's contract address was the zero address.

**`InvalidOperator`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`setOperator` was given the zero address as `operator`.

**`InvalidTokenRange`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
A token-range add or remove had `startTokenId > endTokenId`.

**`NotAuthorized`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
A `*For` function was called by an address that is neither the named `artist` nor an operator the artist approved.

**`TokenAlreadyRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`addToken`/`addTokenFor` targeted a single-token pointer already in the artist's catalog.

**`TokenNotRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`removeToken`/`removeTokenFor` targeted a single-token pointer not in the artist's catalog.

**`TokenRangeAlreadyRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`addTokenRange`/`addTokenRangeFor` targeted a range tuple already in the artist's catalog.

**`TokenRangeNotRegistered`** · [Catalog](/docs/catalog/contracts/catalog#errors)\
`removeTokenRange`/`removeTokenRangeFor` targeted a range tuple not in the artist's catalog.
